summary: Reference matrix for lexer, literal, and diagnostic contracts.

cases.upstream_intent: Common escape sequences stay executable in ordinary string literals.
cases.zr_contract: Valid escape sequences parse and lower without changing surrounding tokenization.
cases.expected_outcome: escaped strings compile and execute with preserved characters
assertions: AST keeps one string literal node
compiled constant preserves escape meaning
runtime result matches expected string

return: escaped strings compile and execute with preserved characters

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Mixed comments and line endings must not destabilize token boundaries.
cases.zr_contract: CRLF and comment adjacency preserve token positions for later diagnostics.
cases.expected_outcome: source with comments and CRLF parses with stable token spans
assertions: line mapping remains monotonic
comment text does not leak into literals
artifact text keeps original statement count

return: source with comments and CRLF parses with stable token spans

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: EOF inside a string literal is a dedicated syntax error.
cases.zr_contract: The parser rejects an unterminated string before later passes observe a completed literal value.
assertions: diagnostic category is stable
line and column point at the broken literal
no completed literal payload reaches SemIR

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Malformed numeric escapes are rejected precisely instead of being normalized silently.
cases.zr_contract: Invalid hex escapes fail in lexical parsing with a dedicated invalid-escape diagnostic.
assertions: diagnostic message contains the bad escape family
range covers only the malformed escape
no fallback character is synthesized

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Unicode escape handling needs boundary coverage around minimum and maximum scalar values.
cases.zr_contract: Valid unicode escapes at supported scalar boundaries normalize consistently and reject unsupported scalar widths.
cases.expected_outcome: supported boundary escapes pass and unsupported widths fail deterministically
assertions: supported boundary value survives roundtrip
unsupported width produces a stable diagnostic family
diagnostic span isolates the escape

exception: supported boundary escapes pass and unsupported widths fail deterministically

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Character literals reject zero-width and multi-codepoint payloads.
cases.zr_contract: Character literals must represent exactly one logical character after escape normalization.
cases.expected_outcome: invalid-width char literals fail before code generation
assertions: char literal diagnostic family is stable
single-character literals still compile
width error does not degrade into generic parse failure

exception: invalid-width char literals fail before code generation

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: A broken literal should not destroy downstream recovery for the rest of the file.
cases.zr_contract: The diagnostic model reports the broken literal while preserving subsequent statement boundaries for follow-up diagnostics.
cases.expected_outcome: first literal error is reported and later declarations remain recoverable
assertions: primary literal diagnostic appears first
secondary statement is still tokenized
artifact or AST count matches recovered statement count

exception: first literal error is reported and later declarations remain recoverable

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Tabs and mixed newlines must not corrupt diagnostic coordinates.
cases.zr_contract: Diagnostic line and column mapping stays stable across tabs, CRLF, and LF within the same file.
cases.expected_outcome: diagnostic range remains stable under mixed newline layouts
assertions: reported line is absolute and stable
reported column does not drift after CRLF
artifact keeps the original source span metadata

exception: diagnostic range remains stable under mixed newline layouts

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Friendly diagnostics depend on precise token and range tracking.
cases.zr_contract: Line and column reporting remain a public contract for literal and tokenization failures.
cases.expected_outcome: diagnostic coordinates remain stable across parser refactors
assertions: line and column are asserted explicitly
diagnostic keyword survives wording drift
project-level compile output prints the same source location

exception: diagnostic coordinates remain stable across parser refactors

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: A dedicated regression keeps EOF literal failures from degrading into generic parser crashes.
cases.zr_contract: EOF inside a literal never escalates to an unrelated parser-internal failure.
cases.expected_outcome: unterminated literal fails cleanly without parser crash
assertions: compiler state stays valid after failure
no runtime artifact is emitted
failure text still names the literal family

exception: unterminated literal fails cleanly without parser crash

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Some reference languages accept alternate multiline literal forms that ZR does not expose.
cases.zr_contract: ZR does not implicitly adopt Lua long strings or Python triple-quoted strings unless the language surface defines them explicitly.
cases.expected_outcome: unsupported multiline literal spellings fail with explicit syntax diagnostics
assertions: diagnostic names the unsupported literal form
parser does not reinterpret the token stream as indexing or calls
project compile path rejects the file identically

exception: unsupported multiline literal spellings fail with explicit syntax diagnostics

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary

cases.upstream_intent: Modern compilers keep diagnosis structured instead of freezing every full message byte-for-byte.
cases.zr_contract: ZR locks error category, source range, and key phrase instead of promising full-message identity for every lexer failure.
cases.expected_outcome: diagnostic assertions target category plus range plus phrase, not full text
assertions: diagnostic helper accepts regex-like phrase matching
artifact tests pin source span metadata
message wording may evolve without breaking the contract

exception: diagnostic assertions target category plus range plus phrase, not full text

parity.skip_reason: binary parity is covered by project/runtime suites; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity optional until manifest-driven parity is wired

binary.skip_reason: project/runtime suites already prove binary execution; this reference case keeps per-case executable parity disabled until the manifest runner can require executed_via=binary
